pages.full_text:
drive to their house.
    Anne Smith kindly gave us buns + crisps + drinks for tea. Then we drove off home.

    August 22nd Saturday.
    Thomas, Peter, John, Charles, Simon + + Tonic Eve, set off at 10. to pick up Dorothy Saner, the German au pair, from Frederik Rd.. William stayed at home to look after Uncle John Crowder.
        We reached Conway at 1 otlod. + had lunch in the carpark, outside the cricket field. We walked along the town walls to the harbour + looked at the boats. The boys fought + fought. Then we had tea at a cross roads below the riding stables, because we could not drop off until 4 at Rhiwiau.
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from the previous page — begins mid-entry. The entry before 'August 22nd Saturday' concludes a previous day's account (Anne Smith giving buns/crisps/drinks). 'otlod' is likely 'o'clock' — partially illegible. 'Tonic' appears to be a nickname (written above the line with a + sign). No year is stated on this page; the right-facing page bears 'Norway Sweden 1998' which is a separate trip overview, so the year of this diary entry cannot be reliably anchored from this page alone. 'Frederik Rd.' could also read 'Frederick Rd.'. 'Rhiwiau' is a place in North Wales.

pages.full_text:
Norway    1998
Sweden
1,500 miles.

[hand-drawn route map of Norway and Sweden with red/pink route line and place labels: Trondheim, Førde, Nigardsbreen, Urnes, Laerdal, Ringebu, Lillehammer, Bergen, Rjukan, Oslo, Kongsberg, Sunne, Karlstad, Setesdal, Stavanger]

1½ ×  [arrow pointing upper-right]
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn outline map of Norway and Sweden with a red/pink line showing the route. The route appears to start at Stavanger (bottom-left, Norway), goes east through Setesdal, then north through Kongsberg, Oslo area, west to Rjukan, then north-west through Laerdal, Urnes, up to Nigardsbreen and Førde, then south to Bergen, then back east through the fjord area, north to Ringebu, Lillehammer, and an arrow at the upper right pointing north-east (indicating onward travel, labelled '1½ ×'). Karlstad and Sunne are marked on the Swedish side (east). The route is drawn in red/pink ink on a black-outline base map.
pages.notes: This page is an overview/summary page for the Norway-Sweden 1998 trip, with a hand-drawn route map. Handwritten page number '19' visible at top-right corner. 'Nigardsbreen' is a glacier in Norway. 'Urnes' likely refers to Urnes Stave Church (UNESCO site). '1½ ×' with an arrow may indicate the map scale or a note about the map enlargement factor. The left-page entry (August 22nd) appears to be from a different, earlier trip (UK/Wales), not the Norway 1998 trip — the two pages of this spread belong to different trips/sections of the diary.
